symptoms.symptom_name: Acute loss of safe mobility / bedbound since discharge
symptoms.onset: over the two days since hospital discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid post-discharge loss of mobility with previously greater independence is the defining feature of deconditioning-driven functional decline.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense fear of falling with situational anxiety, alert and oriented

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fear of falling perpetuates immobility and deconditioning in a self-reinforcing cycle.
Coherent, situationally appropriate anxiety in an alert and oriented patient argues against an acute confusional state.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent discharge after a severe COPD exacerbation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recent exacerbation raises the prior probability of re-exacerbation and mandates an active respiratory screen.
Hospital bed rest and acute illness are the direct cause of the deconditioning.

symptoms.symptom_name: Breathing at baseline, afebrile, no increased/purulent sputum, speaking in full sentences

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No breathlessness above baseline, no fever and no sputum change make an active exacerbation unlikely.
Afebrile with no respiratory deterioration reduces the likelihood of an infective/septic driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: BP 135/85, HR 88, RR 18, Temp 36.7, SpO2 94% (baseline), Wt 82 kg

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Physiologically stable, at-baseline observations confirm no acute medical crisis today — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers stability from the patient/caregiver report, not from a measured vital.
SpO2 at the patient's baseline with a normal respiratory rate is not consistent with an active exacerbation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral knee crepitus with restricted range of motion; painful, stiff knees

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painful osteoarthritic knees mechanically limit standing and walking and compound the deconditioning — the crepitus/ROM findings require an in-person examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unilateral calf swelling, redness or focal tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a unilateral swollen, tender, erythematous calf makes DVT unlikely; generalised leg weakness is not a DVT pattern.
Preserved orientation and coherent conversation argue strongly against delirium.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hazardous home environment — clutter/floor hazards, no bathroom rails

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Environmental hazards in a near-immobile frail elder are a leading modifiable contributor to falls and mandate occupational-therapy home modification.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Physiologically-stable functional crisis with high falls risk
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute functional decline / deconditioning with high falls risk
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute immobility and fear of falling, painful knees and a hazardous home in a person whose acute medical mimics are all screened negative — the pattern that defines a care-coordination need rather than an emergency or a routine deferral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person GP functional and physical assessment (mobility/transfers, respiratory, knees, DVT screen)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive assessment is hands-on and same-day; it confirms physiological stability, excludes acute mimics and quantifies the functional deficit and falls risk. It cannot be done over telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Occupational-therapy in-home hazard and equipment assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies and remediates the home hazards (rails, clutter, over-toilet frame, walker) driving the falls risk.
The patient is afebrile, alert and at respiratory baseline — there is no clinical indication to over-order an infective work-up; doing so would be reflexive over-investigation.
